title: Wrong logic in predicates over views defined with an open-interval predicate
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, value INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK (value >= 0));
INSERT INTO test_table (id, value) VALUES (1, 1), (2, 3), (3, 5), (4, NULL);
CREATE VIEW test_view AS SELECT id, value FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, value FROM test_view WHERE value >= 2 AND value < 5;
SELECT id FROM test_view WHERE value IS NULL OR value < 0;
key_bug_features: Open-interval predicate logic in view definitions
